lint.command: printf 'No dedicated lint command is configured for this repository.\n'
lint.details: No lint configured per mission requirements

nonBlockingIssues.description: Validation regex allows titles starting with numbers but message implies letters only. Path manipulation uses string operations instead of path module. WelcomeScreen has unused onCreateWorktree prop.

deriveSiblingPath uses string manipulation instead of path.dirname(). Collision loop has no safety limit for extreme cases. Git timeouts are hardcoded. Error parsing uses simple string.includes() checks.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the 'rudu/' branch prefix convention as a hard requirement for worktree creation to prevent conflicts with user branches.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features orchestrate-git-worktree-creation-and-first-session and build-welcome-screen-and-create-dialog-shell both use the 'rudu/' prefix pattern. This is a critical namespacing convention that should be explicit.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on proper path handling: prefer path.dirname() over string manipulation for cross-platform compatibility.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviewers noted path manipulation issues in both build-welcome-screen-and-create-dialog-shell (deriveSiblingPath with substring/lastIndexOf) and orchestrate-git-worktree-creation-and-first-session (similar string-based path handling). Both features would benefit from using the standard path module.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the worktreeId linkage pattern (via metadata) as the canonical way to associate sessions with worktrees.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature orchestrate-git-worktree-creation-and-first-session uses worktreeId in session metadata. This pattern should be standardized for future multi-session-per-worktree features.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance about collision handling safety limits and timeout configurability for git operations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature orchestrate-git-worktree-creation-and-first-session implements collision handling but without a safety limit. Git operation timeouts are hardcoded at 5s and 30s. These are implementation details that future workers should consider.
